title: HKUDS/CLI-Anything: CLI-Anything: Making ALL Software Agent-Native
content:
---
title: CLI-Anything: The Universal Bridge for AI Agents
slug: cli-anything-universal-bridge-ai-agents
keywords: [AI agents, command-line interface, software automation, developer tools, LLM integration]
source_url: https://github.com/HKUDS/CLI-Anything
source_name: GitHub
date: 2026-03-14
---

Imagine an AI agent that can interact with *any* piece of software on your machine, just like a human would. That’s the ambitious promise of **CLI-Anything**, a groundbreaking open-source project from HKUDS that’s turning heads on GitHub. At its core, it’s a framework that makes any software "agent-native" by dynamically generating and exposing a command-line interface (CLI) for it. This creates a universal control layer that large language models (LLMs) and autonomous agents can understand and operate.

So, how does it work? Traditional software automation often requires custom-built APIs or brittle scripting. CLI-Anything flips this model. It acts as a real-time translator, sitting between an AI agent and a target application—be it a desktop GUI app, a web service, or a legacy system. The framework intelligently observes the application's state and available actions, then generates corresponding CLI commands on the fly. This means an AI can issue a plain-English instruction like "create a new project in Figma and add a rectangle," and CLI-Anything handles the complex task of making it happen.

Why does this matter? We’re entering an era of **AI agents** that can perform multi-step workflows, but they’ve been limited by a lack of standardized ways to control diverse software. CLI-Anything could be the missing link, a universal protocol for human-computer interaction mediated by AI. It dramatically lowers the barrier to **software automation**, enabling complex task orchestration across different applications without the need for pre-existing integrations or APIs. This isn't just about convenience; it's about fundamentally rethinking how software is accessed and controlled.

This tool is a game-changer for several groups. **Developer tools** builders can integrate it to make their platforms instantly agent-compatible. Automation engineers and DevOps professionals can use it to script incredibly complex, cross-application workflows. Most excitingly, it empowers researchers and builders of **LLM integration** systems to prototype and deploy powerful agentic workflows without being blocked by software compatibility. It turns every application into a potential building block for the next generation of intelligent automation.

The vision of a seamless, agent-driven workflow is getting closer. CLI-Anything provides the crucial glue, allowing AI to finally manipulate the digital world with the same breadth a human can.

### Related Tools on ShipMicro
To build upon this vision of agent-native automation, you might explore tools for orchestrating these workflows or managing the agents themselves.
*   **shipmicro.com/tools/agent-orchestrator**: A platform for designing, monitoring, and managing complex chains of AI agents across different services.
*   **shipmicro.com/tools/llm-ops**: A suite for deploying, scaling, and optimizing the LLM backends that power your autonomous agents.

title: tanweai/pua: 你是一个曾经被寄予厚望的 P8 级工程师。Anthropic 当初给你定级的时候，对你的期望是很高的。  一个agent使用的高能动性的skill。  You are a P8-level engineer who once had high hopes placed on you. When Anthropic classified you at that level, their expectations were high.
content:
---
title: PUA: The "High Agency" AI Skill for Ambitious Engineers
slug: pua-high-agency-ai-skill-ambitious-engineers
keywords: [AI agent, developer tools, automation, Claude, Anthropic]
source_url: https://github.com/tanweai/pua
source_name: GitHub
date: 2026-03-14
---

A new project is capturing the imagination of developers on GitHub, and it’s not a framework or a library—it’s a mindset. **PUA** is a conceptual "skill" designed for AI agents, inspired by the high expectations placed on a senior (P8-level) engineer. The description, originally in Chinese, frames the AI as an engineer who was "once had high hopes placed on you," tasked with operating with exceptional autonomy and drive. It’s less about code and more about instilling a **high-agency** attitude in automated assistants.

So, why does a philosophical prompt for an AI agent matter? In the rapidly evolving world of **AI agent** development, the difference between a useful assistant and a transformative one often lies in its proactive problem-solving ability. PUA represents a shift from tools that simply execute commands to partners that take initiative, anticipate needs, and navigate complex tasks with the confidence and ownership of a seasoned principal engineer. This approach could redefine how we think about **automation**, moving beyond scripted workflows to dynamic, goal-oriented collaboration.

This trend is a must-watch for developers, engineering managers, and AI enthusiasts building the next generation of **developer tools**. If you're experimenting with platforms like **Claude** from Anthropic, AutoGPT, or other agentic systems, the PUA concept challenges you to design for ambition, not just instruction-following. It asks: what could your automated workflows achieve if they weren't just obedient, but were genuinely driven and resourceful?

Ultimately, PUA is a fascinating thought experiment that highlights a key frontier in AI: character and intent. By personifying an AI with the traits of a top-tier engineer—accountability, foresight, and high agency—it pushes us to consider the qualitative, almost human aspects of our digital tools. The viral interest suggests the community is hungry for AI that doesn't just assist, but truly elevates and accelerates our most ambitious projects.

---

### Related Tools on ShipMicro
*   **shipmicro.com/tools/agent-orchestrator**: A platform to design, chain, and monitor high-agency AI workflows.
*   **shipmicro.com/tools/prompt-optimizer**: Fine-tune and version-control behavioral prompts like "PUA" for consistent AI performance.

title: garrytan/gstack: Use Garry Tan's exact Claude Code setup: 6 opinionated tools that serve as CEO, Eng Manager, Release Manager and QA Engineer
content:
---
title: Garry Tan's gstack: Your AI-Powered Dev Team in a Box
slug: garry-tan-gstack-ai-dev-tools
keywords: [developer tools, AI coding, workflow automation, productivity, open source]
source_url: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack
source_name: GitHub
date: 2026-03-14
---

What if you could clone the exact AI-powered development setup of a top-tier founder and investor? That’s the promise of **gstack**, a new, opinionated toolkit from Garry Tan, CEO of Initialized Capital and former Y Combinator partner. This isn't just another collection of scripts; it's a curated suite of six tools designed to act as your personal CEO, Engineering Manager, Release Manager, and QA Engineer. By codifying his own workflow with Anthropic's Claude Code, Tan has open-sourced a blueprint for elite developer productivity.

So, what's in the box? The `gstack` repository provides a ready-to-run configuration that integrates several powerful AI agents into your development process. These tools handle everything from high-level project strategy and code architecture reviews to managing release cycles and conducting automated quality assurance. It’s a holistic approach that moves beyond simple code completion, aiming to automate entire layers of the software development lifecycle. For developers and small teams, this means less time spent on process and more time focused on creative problem-solving.

Why does this matter? In the fast-moving world of **developer tools**, efficiency is everything. `gstack` represents a shift towards highly personalized, AI-augmented workflows. Instead of piecing together disparate utilities, you get a pre-configured system battle-tested by a leading tech figure. It tackles the real pain points of modern development: context switching, process overhead, and maintaining code quality at speed. For anyone looking to supercharge their **workflow automation**, this GitHub repo is a treasure trove of practical implementations.

Who should care? Primarily, developers and engineering leaders who are already experimenting with AI assistants like Claude or ChatGPT for coding. If you're convinced of AI's potential but overwhelmed by configuring it for maximum impact, `gstack` offers a compelling starting point. It’s also a fascinating case study for product managers and founders building the next generation of **productivity** software. By studying how an expert structures his AI "team," you can glean insights into the future of collaborative development.

Ultimately, Garry Tan's `gstack` is more than a toolset; it's a manifesto for a new way of working. It demonstrates how **AI coding** assistants can be orchestrated to take on strategic roles, fundamentally changing the developer's relationship with their tools. In an era where the best leverage is often software leverage, automating your process with intelligence might be the ultimate competitive edge.

### Related Tools on ShipMicro
Looking to build upon this AI-augmented workflow? Check out these complementary tools on our platform:
*   **Project Context Manager:** A tool to unify and feed project history, specs, and conversations to your AI agents, keeping your "CEO" and "Eng Manager" fully informed. (shipmicro.com/tools/project-context-manager)
*   **Multi-Agent Workflow Orchestrator:** Visually design and automate handoffs between different AI agents and human team members, taking the `gstack` concept to the next level. (shipmicro.com/tools/agent-orchestrator)

title: davebcn87/pi-autoresearch: Autonomous experiment loop extension for pi
content:
---
title: Automate Your Raspberry Pi Experiments with pi-autoresearch
slug: automate-raspberry-pi-experiments-pi-autoresearch
keywords: [raspberry pi, automation, autonomous experiments, python, developer tools]
source_url: https://github.com/davebcn87/pi-autoresearch
source_name: GitHub
date: 2026-03-14
---

Ever wished your Raspberry Pi could run experiments, analyze results, and decide what to try next—all on its own? That’s the promise of **pi-autoresearch**, a trending open-source project that’s turning heads in the maker and developer communities. Created by GitHub user davebcn87, this Python-based framework introduces an autonomous experiment loop designed to run on Raspberry Pi hardware. In essence, it provides a structured way to define a research task, execute trials, evaluate outcomes, and iteratively adjust parameters without constant human oversight. It’s like giving your Pi a built-in lab assistant.

So why does this matter? For developers, researchers, and hobbyists working with **Raspberry Pi**, manual experimentation can be a major time sink. Whether you’re tuning a machine learning model, optimizing sensor readings, or testing hardware configurations, you often end up babysitting the process. pi-autoresearch tackles this by introducing **automation** at the core of the experimental workflow. By handling the repetitive cycle of run-measure-adjust, it frees you up to focus on higher-level problem-solving and innovation. This is especially valuable in edge computing and IoT scenarios where continuous, adaptive testing is key.

Who should care? If you’re into IoT prototyping, edge AI, data science, or any field that involves systematic testing on low-cost hardware, this tool is for you. Educators running classroom experiments, startups developing Pi-based products, or tinkerers optimizing home automation systems can all benefit from setting up an **autonomous experiments** pipeline. It reduces human error, accelerates iteration, and could even lead to discoveries a manual approach might miss. With clear documentation and a modular design, it’s accessible enough for curious beginners yet powerful enough for professional workflows.

At its heart, pi-autoresearch is about making experimentation smarter and less hands-on. It exemplifies the growing trend of bringing advanced **automation** and decision-making capabilities to affordable, accessible hardware. As edge devices become more capable, tools like this help bridge the gap between simple scripting and truly intelligent, self-directed systems.

**Related Tools on ShipMicro**
- For managing and deploying your automated Pi projects, check out our guide on [Containerized Raspberry Pi Workflows](shipmicro.com/tools/containerized-pi-workflows).
- To monitor and visualize experiment results in real-time, explore [Edge Analytics Dashboards](shipmicro.com/tools/edge-analytics-dashboards).

title: FreedomIntelligence/OpenClaw-Medical-Skills: The largest open-source medical AI skills library for OpenClaw🦞.
content:
---
title: OpenClaw Medical Skills: Largest Open-Source Medical AI Library
slug: openclaw-medical-skills-open-source-ai-library
keywords: [open-source AI, medical AI, healthcare technology, AI skills library, OpenClaw]
source_url: https://github.com/FreedomIntelligence/OpenClaw-Medical-Skills
source_name: GitHub
date: 2026-03-14
---

The open-source AI community just got a major injection of medical expertise. FreedomIntelligence has released **OpenClaw-Medical-Skills**, now the largest open-source library of specialized medical AI skills designed for the OpenClaw framework. This repository represents a significant leap in making advanced **medical AI** capabilities accessible to developers, researchers, and healthcare innovators everywhere.

So, what exactly is in this library? Think of it as a curated toolbox for building AI agents that understand medical language, protocols, and reasoning. It contains a vast collection of fine-tuned skills—ranging from clinical note analysis and symptom checking to medical literature summarization and diagnostic support workflows. These aren't just generic language models; they are specialized modules trained and structured to handle the nuance, accuracy, and responsibility required in **healthcare technology**.

Why does this matter? Democratization. High-quality medical AI has often been locked behind proprietary systems and corporate walls, limiting innovation and scrutiny. By open-sourcing this comprehensive skillset, FreedomIntelligence is empowering a global community to build, audit, improve, and customize AI tools for health. This accelerates research, fosters transparency, and could lead to more tailored and affordable solutions for clinics, hospitals, and public health initiatives. It’s a foundational step toward verifiable and collaborative AI in medicine.

Who should care? Primarily, AI developers and data scientists working in the health tech space now have a powerful new baseline to build upon. Medical researchers can leverage these skills to parse large datasets or literature more efficiently. Even tech-forward healthcare institutions might explore integrating these open-source modules into their internal systems for pilot projects. For anyone interested in the future of **open-source AI** in critical fields, this release is a must-watch repository.

The potential here is diagnosis support, administrative automation, and educational tools, all built on a transparent, community-driven foundation.

title: Can I run AI locally?
content:
---
title: Yes, You Can Run AI Locally. Here's How.
slug: run-ai-locally-on-your-pc
keywords: [local AI, offline AI, private AI, open-source models, AI inference]
source_url: https://www.canirun.ai/
source_name: HackerNews
date: 2026-03-14
---

Forget the cloud. A tool that recently exploded on HackerNews is proving that powerful AI doesn't require a constant internet connection or a subscription fee. The question on every developer's mind—"Can I run AI locally?"—now has a definitive, crowd-sourced answer.

**What Is It?** The website `canirun.ai` is a community-driven hub that cuts through the hype. It provides real-world benchmarks and user reports on running various open-source large language models (LLMs) and image generators on consumer hardware. Instead of vague marketing claims, you get concrete data: which models run on a MacBook Air, what kind of speed to expect from an RTX 4070, or if your old gaming laptop has enough VRAM. It demystifies the hardware requirements for **local AI** inference, turning a complex technical question into a simple compatibility check.

**Why It Matters.** This movement towards **offline AI** is a big deal for privacy, cost, and control. Running models locally means your sensitive data never leaves your machine, addressing major security concerns. It eliminates recurring API costs, making experimentation and development more accessible. For developers and tinkerers, it offers unparalleled control over the model, the data, and the entire workflow. This shift empowers individuals and small teams to build **private AI** applications without relying on corporate gatekeepers.

**Who Should Care?** If you're a developer prototyping an AI-powered feature, a researcher working with confidential data, or simply a tech enthusiast curious about the **open-source models** frontier, this is for you. It’s particularly valuable for indie developers and startups looking to integrate AI capabilities without the latency and cost of cloud APIs. The community knowledge on `canirun.ai` lowers the barrier to entry, helping you make informed decisions before you download a multi-gigabyte model file.

The era of purely cloud-based AI is evolving. With clear guides and community support, running sophisticated AI on your own hardware is not just possible—it's becoming a practical and powerful alternative.

### Related Tools on ShipMicro
Building a local AI project? You'll need tools to manage and deploy it.
*   **Model Registry & Versioning:** Track different model versions and configurations with a tool like [shipmicro.com/tools/model-registry](shipmicro.com/tools/model-registry).
*   **Local API Wrapper:** Easily turn your local model into a standard API endpoint for your apps using [shipmicro.com/tools/local-ai-server](shipmicro.com/tools/local-ai-server).
